global_style_rules: Every image must be visually consistent with a premium gear brand — think Arc'teryx, Aer, Peak Design
Product texture must be legible: Cordura weave, YKK zipper pulls, Duraflex buckle hardware
No white backgrounds on product shots — only dark navy seamless or real environment
Electric mint (#3FFFAB) appears as a subtle accent or glow — never dominant
People are always in motion or at work — never posed, never smiling at the camera
No stock-photo energy. No text or watermarks. No blurry artefacts

prompts.section: Hero — primary product image
prompts.purpose: Above-the-fold hero shot. This is the first impression of the brand and its most important asset for conversion.
prompts.emotional_role: Desire and credibility — the viewer must immediately feel 'that is a serious, beautiful piece of gear'
prompts.commercial_role: This single image must generate enough trust and desire to drive a click to 'Build Your Pack'
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Core Pack modular backpack — hero product shot
prompts.short_prompt: Premium studio photo of a modern black modular backpack with electric mint accents, dark navy background, magazine-quality product photography.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A high-end product photography shot of the TrailVolt Core Pack in Core Black. The pack is positioned at a 30-degree 3/4 angle on a dark navy (#0B1626) seamless background. Cinematic rim lighting from upper-left creates sharp definition on the 600D ballistic nylon shell — the weave texture is clearly visible. A subtle electric mint glow (#3FFFAB) emanates from behind the pack, as if from a distant studio light source on the floor. The pack shows: precise YKK zipper pulls in matte black, reinforced module attachment points on the front face (the magnetic/mechanical connection rail, a defining product feature), padded shoulder strap visible on one side, rigid structure of the 22L form factor. Camera is at eye level. Depth of field is shallow. Dark navy gradient background. Studio quality, ultra-sharp, 8K, no text, no watermark, no hands, no people.
prompts.negative_prompt: white background, hands, people, watermark, text, blurry, plastic-looking hardware, cheap zippers, generic bag, cartoon, illustration, logo overlay, bright unnatural colours
prompts.style_notes: This must feel like a Gear Patrol hero image or a Peak Design product reveal. Not an Amazon listing. A statement.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The hero image is the commercial centre of the homepage. Without it, the page has no product identity. It must communicate premium quality, modularity, and the dark navy brand world in one frame.

prompts.section: Three Lines section — Everyday line card
prompts.purpose: Lifestyle image for the Everyday 22L urban commuter line card on the homepage
prompts.emotional_role: Aspirational urban professionalism — this is what your Monday morning could look like
prompts.commercial_role: Drive clicks to product.html?line=everyday by making the Everyday line feel relevant to a professional city person
prompts.alt_text: Person commuting in Dublin city with TrailVolt Everyday 22L Core Black backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Professional person walking confidently through a Dublin morning street with a sleek black modular backpack, cinematic natural light, motion-forward composition.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic lifestyle photo of an urban professional (late 20s–early 40s, gender-neutral or male, dressed smartly-casual — dark jacket, quality trainers) walking forward through a Dublin city street in early morning light. The TrailVolt Everyday 22L in Core Black is on their back, clearly visible — the front module attachment face is distinguishable, the structured form holds its shape. The environment: Georgian terrace facades, a wet Dublin morning pavement, soft golden-hour light coming from the right. The person is mid-stride, purposeful — not posing. Background is bokeh with suggestion of other commuters and architecture. Cinematic, slightly desaturated warm tones. Real photography aesthetic — editorial, not commercial.
prompts.negative_prompt: posed smiling, direct camera eye contact, studio setup, white background, generic stock photo, bright saturated colours, fake sunshine, mountains, trails, airport
prompts.style_notes: This is the city card — Dublin morning commute energy. Montserrat-weight confidence in the subject.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The Everyday line exists because TrailVolt's founder used to repack from his work bag every Friday. This image is the resolution of that frustration — the one person who doesn't need to switch bags at the office door.

prompts.section: Three Lines section — Travel line card
prompts.purpose: Lifestyle image for the Travel 30L cabin-ready line card
prompts.emotional_role: Freedom and readiness — you're the person at the gate who doesn't need to check a bag
prompts.commercial_role: Communicate that the Travel 30L replaces luggage for most trips. Drive configure intent.
prompts.alt_text: Traveller at airport gate with TrailVolt Travel 30L modular backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Confident traveller at an airport departure gate with a structured black travel backpack, golden evening light through terminal windows, no checked baggage.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic travel lifestyle photo of a confident person (late 20s–late 30s) standing at an airport departure gate lounge, large floor-to-ceiling terminal windows behind them showing an evening sky. The TrailVolt Travel 30L in Core Black is slung over one shoulder — the structured clamshell form is visible, clearly a 30L cabin bag, not a hiking pack. The person is standing, looking through the window — they are ready, calm, minimal. No trolley, no checked luggage. The clamshell structure of the Travel pack subtly communicates 'this is it — my whole trip'. Warm ambient terminal light. Cinematic, slightly warm colour grade. Editorial travel photography aesthetic.
prompts.negative_prompt: crowds, busy airport chaos, luggage carousel, checked bags, stress, posed for camera, mountains, trails, office environment, white studio
prompts.style_notes: This is calm, confident travel. The person travels light — not because they compromised, but because the bag is that capable.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The site copy says 'This is the only luggage you need for most trips.' This image is the visual proof of that claim.

prompts.section: Three Lines section — Trail line card
prompts.purpose: Lifestyle image for the Trail 28L all-terrain line card
prompts.emotional_role: Weekend energy — the call to go further, to be in terrain, to carry gear that matches the environment
prompts.commercial_role: Communicate durability and adventure capability. Drive trail-focused buyers to configure the Trail 28L.
prompts.alt_text: Hiker on Irish mountain trail at golden hour with TrailVolt Trail 28L Forest Green backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Hiker moving along a misty Irish or Scottish mountain ridge at golden hour, Forest Green modular trail pack loaded and in use, dramatic green hills and moody sky.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A dramatic outdoor lifestyle photograph of a hiker (30s, capable, focused) moving along a mountain trail in the Irish highlands — Wicklow Mountains or Wild Atlantic Way coastal terrain. The TrailVolt Trail 28L in Forest Green is on their back — loaded, compression straps pulled taut, bottle holder module visible on one side with a water bottle. The pack's load-distribution harness is visible across the shoulders. Golden hour light breaks through cloud over the hills. Mist sits in the valley below. The hiker is mid-step on rough terrain — not posing, not resting. The Cordura shell texture is visible and wet-looking from Irish weather. Cinematic wide crop, dramatic landscape, editorial adventure photography.
prompts.negative_prompt: studio, city, airport, commute, white background, bright sunshine, tropical location, posed, smiling at camera, generic hiking stock photo
prompts.style_notes: Wild Atlantic Way meets Arc'teryx campaign. The pack is functional and in use — this isn't recreation, it's preparation.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Tribe member Aoife Murphy hiked the Wild Atlantic Way with her Trail 28L for six days. This image is her story — and every potential trail buyer's aspiration.

prompts.section: Modular System Explainer — product concept diagram
prompts.purpose: Explains the core product innovation: one base pack + eight snap-on modules
prompts.emotional_role: Understanding and excitement — the viewer should feel 'I finally get it' and 'I want that'
prompts.commercial_role: The modular concept is the primary competitive differentiator. This image must communicate it visually without requiring copy.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt modular attachment system — Core Pack with all eight modules arranged around it
prompts.short_prompt: Product concept diagram: black modular backpack at centre with eight accessories orbiting around it in a dark navy field, electric mint connection lines, premium technical illustration.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A premium product concept layout shot: the TrailVolt Core Pack in Core Black is centred on a dark navy (#0B1626) background. Around it, arranged in a clean circular or radial pattern, are all 8 modules — slightly separated from the pack, as if hovering just before attachment. The module connection points on the front face of the pack emit a subtle electric mint (#3FFFAB) glow. Thin mint-coloured lines or arrows connect each module to its corresponding attachment point on the pack. The modules are identifiable: chest phone holder, tech organizer pouch, power bank unit, shoe compartment bag, clothing cube, slim document sleeve, side bottle holder, rain cover pouch. All items are premium product photography quality, sharp, matte-finished hardware. Dark background, no text, no labels, studio product lighting. Think: Apple product launch diagram meets Gear Patrol product review layout.
prompts.negative_prompt: white background, text labels, low quality, cluttered arrangement, generic accessories, stock photo props, bright colours other than mint accents
prompts.style_notes: This is the explainer image. It must be clear at a glance — the pack is the sun, modules are planets. One image = the entire product system.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The site's modular section says 'snap on what the day demands, leave behind what it doesn't.' This image is the visual proof of that promise — and it appears above the four-step process explanation.

prompts.section: Everyday Line — Core Black product card (Best Seller)
prompts.purpose: Shop card product image for Everyday 22L in Core Black — the site's best-selling configuration
prompts.emotional_role: This is the one everyone wants. It must look immediately desirable.
prompts.commercial_role: Best seller label means this drives highest conversion volume. The image must close the sale.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Everyday 22L Core Black modular backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a structured matte black 22L modular backpack on a dark navy background, cinematic lighting, visible YKK zippers and module attachment points.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Clean product photography of the TrailVolt Everyday 22L pack in Core Black on a dark navy to dark charcoal gradient background. The pack is displayed front-facing or at a slight 3/4 angle. The 600D ballistic nylon shell shows visible weave texture under sharp lighting. The front face shows the module attachment rail clearly — a distinguishing feature of TrailVolt packs. YKK zipper pulls are matte black and precise. The structured 22L form holds its shape without slouching. Shoulder straps visible at the top. The laptop compartment back panel padding is visible on the back panel if shown. Cinematic lighting: main light from upper-right, rim from left edge. No text, no hands, no people. Studio quality, sharp, commercial product photography.
prompts.negative_prompt: white background, people, hands, generic bag without module face, cheap zippers, slouching form, blurry, grey or coloured background other than dark navy
prompts.style_notes: This is the best seller. It must look like the pack people keep buying again for gifts.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The site copy: 'The essential daily configuration. The one they always ask about.' The image must match that confidence.

prompts.section: Everyday Line — Slate Grey product card
prompts.purpose: Shop card for Everyday 22L in Slate Grey — the office-appropriate, understated colourway
prompts.emotional_role: Versatility and restraint — the person who doesn't want to stand out but still wants quality
prompts.commercial_role: Colour variant upsell. Must look genuinely distinct from Core Black — not just 'grey version'.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Everyday 22L Slate Grey modular backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a slate grey modular backpack on a dark navy background, understated, office-ready, visible module attachment system.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Clean product photography of the TrailVolt Everyday 22L in Slate Grey on a dark navy background. The pack's grey colour reads as a warm mid-grey — not cold, not dark. The 600D ballistic nylon texture is visible. The module attachment face on the front is visible. YKK zipper pulls are in a slightly lighter grey tone. The overall pack looks polished and office-appropriate — it would look at home in a Soho meeting room and a Friday afternoon. Cinematic studio lighting, same framing as the Core Black variant (3/4 or front facing), equally sharp. The colour difference from Core Black is immediately legible — this is clearly a different, lighter colourway. No text, no people, no watermarks.
prompts.negative_prompt: white background, Core Black confusion, same colourway as hero image, hands, people, text, cheap hardware, generic styling
prompts.style_notes: Should feel like the intelligent professional's choice — a considered colour, not a safe default.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'Understated, versatile, and office-ready.' The image must communicate exactly that in a single frame.

prompts.section: Everyday Line — Forest Green product card (Limited)
prompts.purpose: Shop card for Everyday 22L in Forest Green — limited colourway, highest urgency positioning
prompts.emotional_role: Desire through scarcity — this is the one that sells out. It must look special.
prompts.commercial_role: Limited label creates urgency. The forest green must look like a deliberate premium colour choice, not an odd leftover.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Everyday 22L Forest Green limited modular backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a deep forest green modular backpack on a dark navy background, limited colourway, premium feel, visible YKK hardware and module attachment face.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Everyday 22L in Forest Green on a dark navy seamless background. The Forest Green is a deep, muted, confident shade — approximately #2D5016 — rich and natural without being bright or garish. The green is visible and legible against the dark navy background without competing. The same module attachment face visible on the front. YKK zipper pulls in dark metal or black. The pack's structured 22L form. Cinematic lighting brings out the subtle sheen of the ballistic nylon in this colour. The overall impression: this is a considered, adult outdoor colour — what you'd expect from a brand like Cotopaxi or Arc'teryx, not a sports shop. No text, no people.
prompts.negative_prompt: bright lime green, cartoon green, white background, people, hands, cheap hardware, neon accents
prompts.style_notes: The forest green must feel outdoors-adjacent even in a studio shot — it has a 'weekend personality' even in an office context.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'A subtle nod to the outdoors for your daily carry. Limited colourway — sells out fast.' The image must make someone feel they'd regret not buying it.

prompts.section: Travel Line — Core Black product card (Cabin Ready)
prompts.purpose: Shop card for Travel 30L in Core Black — the cabin-ready travel configuration
prompts.emotional_role: Competence and freedom — this is the pack of someone who never loses their luggage
prompts.commercial_role: Drive Travel line sales. The pack must visually communicate its IATA compliance and clamshell capability.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Travel 30L Core Black IATA cabin-compliant backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a structured black 30L travel backpack with clamshell profile and modular front face, dark background, premium travel gear aesthetics.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Travel 30L in Core Black. The pack is clearly larger and more structured than the Everyday 22L — the 30L volume is evident in the taller, deeper profile. The clamshell flat-open design capability is hinted at by the wrap-around zipper visible on the main compartment. The lockable zipper pulls are visible. The ripstop nylon shell (420D) has a slightly different texture to the Everyday ballistic nylon — finer grain, slightly more uniform. The module attachment face is visible on the front. Dark navy background, cinematic rim lighting. The overall impression is: this is a serious travel bag, not a hiking pack — the profile is structured and travel-optimized. No text, no people.
prompts.negative_prompt: hiking pack shape, dome top, white background, people, hands, casual-looking hardware, budget travel aesthetics
prompts.style_notes: This should look like it fits perfectly in an overhead bin — that is the product's core promise.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'Built for the modern nomad. Pack it once. Unpack at the destination.' Tribe member Marco used his Travel for 14 countries, never checked.

prompts.section: Travel Line — Slate Grey product card (Most Popular)
prompts.purpose: Most popular travel configuration — Slate Grey carries connotations of business and versatility
prompts.emotional_role: The smart traveller's choice — appropriate for both business meetings and weekend escapes
prompts.commercial_role: 'Most Popular' label — this converts through social proof. The image must look premium and versatile.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Travel 30L Slate Grey most popular configuration
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a slate grey 30L structured travel backpack, neutral and refined, dark background, premium materials visible.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Travel 30L in Slate Grey. The slate grey colourway on 420D ripstop nylon has a slightly different texture quality to the Everyday ballistic nylon in the same colour — finer, more uniform, slightly more technical-looking. The structured travel profile is clear. Module attachment face visible on the front. Lockable zipper pulls. Cinematic dark navy studio lighting. The slate grey reads as 'neutral enough for business, capable enough for the trail' — which is the exact product promise. No text, no hands.
prompts.negative_prompt: dark background causing greyed-out colourway confusion, white background, people, hands, text, generic styling
prompts.style_notes: The Slate Grey Travel pack is described as 'most popular' — it should look universally appealing and aspirationally professional.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'Neutral enough for business, spacious enough for the weekend.' The image carries both meanings simultaneously.

prompts.section: Travel Line — Custom Build card
prompts.purpose: Represents the custom configuration option — a call to build from scratch
prompts.emotional_role: Personalisation and ownership — this is the pack that's only yours
prompts.commercial_role: Drive users to the product configurator. The 'Custom' framing is about creative ownership.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Travel 30L custom build configuration
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a Travel 30L backpack shown partially assembled — some modules attached, connection points glowing mint — representing a build in progress.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Travel 30L showing a 'custom build in progress' concept. The base pack (in Core Black or a mixed colourway suggesting customisation) is on the dark navy background. Two or three modules are attached to the front face — visible attachment points, one module slightly offset as if just being snapped on. The module connection points emit a subtle electric mint glow as if responding to the magnetic snap. The image suggests 'your configuration, your way' — it's not a finished product shot but a concept of personalisation. Clean, cinematic studio lighting. No people or hands needed — the suggestion of personalisation is in the composition.
prompts.negative_prompt: completed product shot identical to other cards, hands reaching in awkwardly, messy composition, too many props, white background
prompts.style_notes: This is the 'build yours' card — it should feel like an invitation, not just another product photo.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The configurator page is a key conversion point. This card leads directly to it.

prompts.section: Trail Line — Core Black product card (Versatile)
prompts.purpose: Shop card for Trail 28L in Core Black — the all-terrain pack in an urban-friendly colourway
prompts.emotional_role: The trail runner who also catches Monday flights — this is for them
prompts.commercial_role: The Core Black Trail bridges outdoor and urban — broadens the Trail line's audience beyond pure hikers.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Trail 28L Core Black all-terrain modular backpack
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a matte black 28L trail backpack with Cordura texture, load-distribution harness, dark background, performance outdoor aesthetics.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Trail 28L in Core Black. The 500D Cordura nylon shell has a distinctly different texture to the Everyday ballistic nylon — coarser, more rugged-looking military weave. The load-distribution harness is visible — padded shoulder straps with adjustment points, sternum strap connection point. The DWR-coated shell has a slight water-resistant surface quality visible in the lighting. The hydration sleeve rear access point is subtly visible. The module attachment face on the front shows the connection system. Overall: this pack looks more robust than the Everyday, with reinforced details and a slightly wider, more functional profile. Dark navy background, cinematic lighting. No text, no people.
prompts.negative_prompt: sleek office-bag profile, skinny straps, no harness, white background, generic pack shape, hiking pole clips as main feature
prompts.style_notes: This should look like it could handle a Wicklow Way weekend and still not look out of place at a Dublin airport on Monday.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'All the performance of the Trail line in a clean, urban-friendly colourway.' Tribe member Tomás runs trails on weekends and catches flights on Mondays.

prompts.section: Trail Line — Forest Green product card (Trail Favourite)
prompts.purpose: The signature Trail colourway — the one the Trail community identifies with
prompts.emotional_role: This is the outdoor person's pack. It looks like it belongs on a mountain.
prompts.commercial_role: Highest relevance for trail-focused buyers. Drives Trail line purchases in the iconic colourway.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Trail 28L Forest Green signature colourway
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a deep forest green 28L trail backpack with Cordura weave, load harness, and outdoor ruggedness, against a dark background.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Trail 28L in Forest Green — the signature colourway of the Trail line. The deep forest green (#2D5016) on 500D Cordura has a matte, slightly earthy quality — it reads as 'serious outdoor gear.' The Cordura weave texture is clearly visible, coarser than ballistic nylon. Load-distribution harness visible on the shoulder straps. Compression straps pulled moderately tight as if the pack is loaded. The DWR coating gives the shell a slight surface texture that reads as weatherproof. Module attachment face visible on the front. Dark navy background — the forest green stands out confidently. Cinematic outdoor-referencing lighting with slight warmer tone to evoke nature context. No text, no people.
prompts.negative_prompt: bright lime green, cheap camping-store green, white background, generic outdoor bag, no harness, urban profile
prompts.style_notes: This is the Trail line's identity image. The Forest Green must look like a deliberate, premium outdoor colour choice.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Tribe member Aoife's Rain Cover quote, the Wild Atlantic Way story — this is the colourway of the TrailVolt community's outdoor stories.

prompts.section: Trail Line — Slate Grey product card (New)
prompts.purpose: New addition to the Trail line — Slate Grey brings a technical aesthetic to the outdoors
prompts.emotional_role: Technical sophistication — this is for the person who wants performance without aggressive styling
prompts.commercial_role: New product card — must look fresh and deliberate, not like a leftover colourway.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Trail 28L Slate Grey new technical colourway
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product shot of a slate grey 28L trail backpack with Cordura texture, technical aesthetic, dark background, performance gear energy.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Trail 28L in Slate Grey — a new addition to the Trail family. The slate grey Cordura has a distinctly technical quality — more structured and deliberate than the Forest Green, less 'traditional' outdoor. The coarse 500D weave is visible in the grey colourway. Load-distribution harness visible. Hydration sleeve access point visible. The Bottle Holder module can be optionally visible on the side. Dark navy background, clean cinematic lighting. The grey reads as 'technical outdoor' — it could work both on trail and in an airport. No text, no people.
prompts.negative_prompt: cold or blue-toned grey, white background, urban-only feeling, no visible harness, generic product
prompts.style_notes: Site copy suggests pairing with Bottle Holder and Rain Cover — those details can be visible.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: New product introduction — the Slate Grey Trail bridges the gap between Core Black's urban versatility and Forest Green's outdoor identity.

prompts.section: Product Configurator — default line image (Everyday)
prompts.purpose: The main visual shown in the configurator when Everyday 22L is selected — must render beautifully at 500x560
prompts.emotional_role: Confidence in choice — the user has selected their line and this image confirms it looks great
prompts.commercial_role: The configurator visual drives add-to-cart conversion. It must look premium and confirm the choice.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Core Pack Everyday 22L — configurator preview image
prompts.short_prompt: Hero product shot of TrailVolt Everyday 22L Core Black, front-facing, dark navy background, ultra sharp, configurator display quality.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A definitive product photography image of the TrailVolt Core Pack Everyday 22L in Core Black, designed for display in the product configurator at 500x560px. The pack is displayed front-facing or at a very slight 3/4 angle (≤15 degrees) — all features of the front face are fully visible: module attachment rail, front compartment zipper, top carry handle, shoulder strap attachment points. 600D ballistic nylon shell texture clearly visible. YKK zipper pulls in matte black. Electric mint glow accent at the base or behind the pack. The image is taller than it is wide, filling the frame — the pack occupies 75% of the frame height. Dark navy background fading to near-black at edges. Ultra-sharp. This is the product image users see while choosing their configuration — it must confirm premium quality at close inspection range.
prompts.negative_prompt: cropped straps, cut-off top or bottom, tilt over 20 degrees, white background, people, hands, lifestyle context, other bags visible
prompts.style_notes: This is the configurator hero — it must look sharp at full size in a product panel. Think Apple Store product imagery.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The product.html configurator page drives direct conversion to checkout. The visual panel image is the last thing a user looks at before adding to cart.

prompts.section: Product Configurator — Travel line image
prompts.purpose: Configurator visual shown when Travel 30L is selected
prompts.emotional_role: Validation — the user sees the Travel 30L and knows it looks right
prompts.commercial_role: Reinforces the premium nature of the Travel line's larger form factor during the configuration decision
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Core Pack Travel 30L — configurator preview image
prompts.short_prompt: Hero product shot of TrailVolt Travel 30L Core Black with clamshell profile visible, front-facing, tall format, dark background, premium travel backpack.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A definitive product photography image of the TrailVolt Travel 30L in Core Black for the product configurator. The pack is clearly larger than the Everyday — the 30L volume creates a taller, slightly deeper profile. The wrap-around clamshell zipper is visible along the edge of the main compartment — a key Travel-line feature. Lockable YKK zipper pulls. 420D ripstop nylon texture (finer grain than Everyday's ballistic nylon). Module attachment face visible on the front. Tall 5:5.6 crop fills the frame. Dark navy background. Ultra-sharp configurator quality. The pack is positioned front-facing or ≤15 degree 3/4 angle. No people, no text.
prompts.negative_prompt: hiking dome top, frame pack shape, white background, identical to Everyday 22L image, people, hands
prompts.style_notes: The Travel line should read 'airport-ready' even in this studio image — the clamshell structure is the tell.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Travel is IATA cabin-compliant. The configurator image must communicate 'this goes in the overhead bin' visually.

prompts.section: Product Configurator — Trail line image
prompts.purpose: Configurator visual shown when Trail 28L is selected
prompts.emotional_role: Performance confirmation — the user sees the Trail 28L and believes it can handle their next trip
prompts.commercial_role: Trail line is the premium technical configuration. The image must justify its reputation.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Core Pack Trail 28L — configurator preview image
prompts.short_prompt: Hero product shot of TrailVolt Trail 28L Core Black with Cordura weave, load harness, hydration sleeve access, tall format, dark background, performance outdoor backpack.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A definitive product photography image of the TrailVolt Trail 28L in Core Black for the product configurator. The 500D Cordura shell has a clearly coarser, more rugged texture than the other lines. The load-distribution harness is prominently visible — padded shoulder straps with technical webbing, sternum strap visible at chest height. The hydration sleeve access point (rear) is visible. The reinforced base is discernible at the bottom. The 28L profile is robust and structural — wider stance than the Everyday. Module attachment face on the front. Tall 5:5.6 crop. Dark navy background. Ultra-sharp. No people, no text.
prompts.negative_prompt: office bag profile, thin straps without harness system, white background, identical to Everyday image, overpacked appearance, people, hands
prompts.style_notes: The Trail line must look most technical and rugged of the three — that is its identity.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Trail line features: DWR coating, 3L hydration sleeve, load-distribution harness. The image must show these are visible and deliberate design choices.

prompts.section: About Hero — founder portrait
prompts.purpose: Portrait of Ruslan, the founder of TrailVolt, in the Dublin design studio — brand trust builder
prompts.emotional_role: Authenticity and connection — this is a real person who solved a real problem
prompts.commercial_role: Founder portrait drives brand trust and converts sceptical buyers by humanising the brand
prompts.alt_text: Ruslan, founder of TrailVolt, in the Dublin design studio
prompts.short_prompt: Confident founder portrait of a man in his 30s in a clean industrial design studio in Dublin, TrailVolt backpack visible in background, natural window light, candid editorial photography.
prompts.detailed_prompt: An editorial founder portrait of Ruslan (male, Eastern European features, 30s, sharp but relaxed appearance — a builder, not a model) in a clean Dublin design studio or workshop. He is not looking directly at the camera — instead looking at a prototype on a table, or looking out a window. He is dressed in smart-casual style — dark crewneck, quality trousers, a considered person. A TrailVolt Core Pack in Core Black is visible in the background on a shelf or table. Workshop context: design drawings or fabric swatches on the table, a MacBook, a prototype. Natural north-facing light from large studio windows. The image feels real — like it was taken during an actual work session. Not posed, not corporate. Editorial quality — the kind of image that would appear in WIRED or Monocle.
prompts.negative_prompt: corporate headshot, forced smile, suit and tie, sterile white background, studio lights visible, generic office environment, hands on hips pose
prompts.style_notes: The page says 'Built Out of Frustration. Perfected by Obsession.' The portrait must show the obsession without the frustration — the resolved, focused version.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The entire about.html page is Ruslan's story. The hero section leads with his portrait — it must make visitors trust the brand through him.

prompts.section: Photo grid — early prototype testing in Wicklow Mountains
prompts.purpose: Documentary-style photo of Ruslan testing an early TrailVolt prototype in the Wicklow Mountains
prompts.emotional_role: Proof of real-world development — this pack was tested on actual terrain, not in a lab
prompts.commercial_role: Reinforces the 'minimum six physical prototyping rounds' credibility claim
prompts.alt_text: Ruslan testing early TrailVolt prototype in the Wicklow Mountains
prompts.short_prompt: Man testing an early backpack prototype on a misty Wicklow Mountain trail, Irish highland scenery, documentary photography style, natural light, rugged terrain.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A documentary-style vertical portrait of a man (30s, focused, appropriately dressed for a mountain day — technical jacket, walking boots) testing an early prototype of the TrailVolt Core Pack on a trail in the Wicklow Mountains, Ireland. The prototype pack looks slightly rougher than the finished product — visible test stitching, maybe a protruding module connection point being evaluated. The Wicklow terrain is unmistakable: rolling green hills, peat-dark paths, low cloud or mist, scattered gorse. The man is walking or standing looking at the pack's attachment points — actively evaluating it. Golden hour or overcast Irish day lighting. The image should feel raw and authentic — a real product test, not a campaign shoot. Vertical 3:4 crop.
prompts.negative_prompt: finished polished product, sunny blue sky, foreign landscape, posed for camera, tropical or alpine scenery, studio or urban background
prompts.style_notes: This is the 'proof' image — showing real-world testing in real Irish conditions.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The about page notes 'Tested in commutes, airports, hiking trails, and everyday Dublin weather.' Wicklow is the test environment — this is the visual evidence.

prompts.section: Photo grid — early workshop and prototype stage
prompts.purpose: Documents the early TrailVolt prototyping stage — materials spread on a workshop table
prompts.emotional_role: Craftsmanship origin — this brand was made by hand, piece by piece
prompts.commercial_role: Justifies premium pricing: this is not a drop-shipped product, it was built with intentionality
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt prototypes spread on workshop table, early development stage
prompts.short_prompt: Overhead or side view of early TrailVolt backpack prototypes spread on a workshop table, fabric swatches, YKK zipper samples, design sketches, warm workshop light.
prompts.detailed_prompt: An intimate workshop documentation photo showing an early prototyping session. On a raw wood or steel workshop table: two or three TrailVolt prototype packs in various stages of assembly — some partially built, showing internal structure, some complete but with exposed stitching. Surrounding them: fabric swatches in Cordura, ballistic nylon, and ripstop nylon; YKK zipper samples in different sizes; Duraflex buckle hardware samples; sketched diagrams on graph paper or design sheets; a measuring tape; possibly scissors or a seam ripper. Warm incandescent workshop light. The image is slightly cluttered in a purposeful way — this is a working session. Vertical 3:4 crop. The feeling: honest, hands-on, early-stage craft.
prompts.negative_prompt: finished polished product, clean empty table, stock prop styling, IKEA-style setup, bright colours, studio flash photography, modern tech office
prompts.style_notes: This should look like a real product development session — Jony Ive's sketchbook, not an Instagram flat-lay.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The brand promise includes 'Obsessive quality over seasonal volume.' This image shows the obsession during the development phase.

prompts.section: Photo grid — Dublin Airport, Travel line prototype in use
prompts.purpose: Documents the origin moment — Ruslan at Dublin Airport, the inspiration for TrailVolt's modular concept
prompts.emotional_role: Origin and narrative — this is the exact location where the idea was born
prompts.commercial_role: Grounds the brand story in a real moment and real problem (repacking at airports)
prompts.alt_text: Ruslan boarding a flight at Dublin Airport with Travel 30L prototype
prompts.short_prompt: Person at Dublin Airport departure terminal with a structured travel backpack, gate boarding moment, cinematic travel photography, no checked luggage.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic vertical travel photograph of a man (30s) at Dublin Airport — recognisable from the distinct Dublin Airport terminal architecture: the glass ceiling, the green departure board, the bright white terminal gates. He is standing at or near a gate, a TrailVolt Travel 30L prototype on his back — the only bag he has. He is looking up at a departure board or looking ahead toward a gate — not at the camera. The Travel 30L is clearly a single bag: no roller bag, no checked luggage, just the pack. The image captures the freedom of the moment: this person has everything they need in one bag. Terminal ambient light — bright, slightly blown-out whites in the background contrasting with the dark navy pack. Cinematic vertical crop.
prompts.negative_prompt: stress, luggage carousel, checked bags, generic airport stock photo, suburban terminal, dark or underground setting
prompts.style_notes: The site origin story: 'I was at Dublin Airport — Everyday backpack on my back, wishing it were a travel bag.' This image is after that moment was solved.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The Travel line's core promise is born from this exact scenario. The photo grid must show the founder living the solution.

prompts.section: Our Process section — workshop craftsmanship image
prompts.purpose: Main process image: stitching in progress, craftsperson precision, TrailVolt quality evidence
prompts.emotional_role: Trust in material quality — you see the hands that make the product
prompts.commercial_role: 'Premium materials only' is a core brand claim. This image makes that claim visceral.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt workshop, prototype materials laid out, stitching in progress
prompts.short_prompt: Close-up of a craftsperson guiding dark Cordura fabric under an industrial sewing machine, sharp stitch line, warm workshop light, quality craftsmanship photography.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A focused craftsmanship photograph in a TrailVolt workshop setting. Close-up or medium close-up of skilled hands guiding dark navy or black Cordura nylon under an industrial sewing machine (Juki or similar professional grade). The stitch line is precise — a clean, tight seam visible in the frame. The YKK zipper tape is being stitched into the fabric edge. Warm, overhead workshop light catches the metallic needle and the sheen of the nylon. In soft focus behind: spools of thread in black and dark grey, perhaps a measuring tape or pattern piece. The hands belong to a craftsperson who clearly knows what they're doing — confident, precise grip. The image conveys: this is made deliberately, by people who care. Wide/landscape 7:6 format.
prompts.negative_prompt: factory automation, robot machinery, cheap materials, loose stitching, bright white sterile environment, stock photography of random sewing
prompts.style_notes: This is the process proof — the visual evidence that 'premium materials only' is real.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Our Process section describes 4 steps; the process image must embody step 3: 'Premium Materials Only. We source Cordura, ballistic nylon, YKK zippers, and Duraflex hardware.'

prompts.section: Longevity by Design — repair kit visual
prompts.purpose: Visual representation of the TrailVolt repair kit — YKK zipper pulls, Duraflex buckles, thread, module connectors
prompts.emotional_role: Care and longevity — this brand stands behind its product for life
prompts.commercial_role: Supports 'Designed for Repair' commitment — a key sustainability differentiator and objection handler
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt spare parts repair kit — YKK zipper pull, Duraflex buckle, module connector, thread
prompts.short_prompt: Premium flat-lay of repair kit components on a dark surface: YKK zipper pull, Duraflex buckle, thread spool, module connector hardware, electric mint accent lighting.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A deliberate, premium flat-lay product photograph of the TrailVolt spare parts repair kit on a dark navy or slate surface. The items are arranged with intention: a YKK zipper pull in matte black (the distinct YKK brand profile is recognisable), a Duraflex buckle in black polypropylene (the architectural form of the buckle visible), a spool of matching dark thread, a module connector piece (showing the stainless steel and nylon magnetic attachment hardware), and optionally a small sewing needle and spare cordage. Subtle electric mint accent lighting from below or the side catches the metallic hardware pieces. The arrangement is sparse and considered — these are not cluttered, they are curated. The overall impression: someone cares enough about this product to supply parts for its entire life. Wide/landscape format. No text, no people.
prompts.negative_prompt: messy scattered parts, bright colours, generic hardware store look, cartoon styling, white background, unbranded hardware
prompts.style_notes: This should feel like the opposite of disposability — a promise materialised as objects.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: 'We stock spare parts indefinitely for every product we've ever made.' This image is the visual proof of that commitment.

prompts.section: Tribe member cards — Aoife Murphy, Adventure Hiker, Donegal
prompts.purpose: Portrait/lifestyle card for Aoife — hiking the Wild Atlantic Way with Trail 28L
prompts.emotional_role: Community validation — a real hiker chose this pack for a serious journey
prompts.commercial_role: Drives Trail 28L + Rain Cover Module purchase through community testimonial
prompts.alt_text: Aoife Murphy, Adventure Hiker, Donegal — TrailVolt Tribe member
prompts.short_prompt: Irish woman hiker in her late 20s on the Wild Atlantic Way cliffs, Trail 28L green backpack, rain cover module deployed, windswept landscape, cinematic horizon shot.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A wide cinematic portrait (2:1) of Aoife — a woman in her late 20s to early 30s, outdoorsy, strong, Irish — standing or walking on the clifftops of the Wild Atlantic Way, Donegal. The TrailVolt Trail 28L in Forest Green is on her back with the Rain Cover Module fully deployed — the translucent rain cover pulled over the pack. Windswept hair. Practical outdoor clothing: waterproof jacket, hiking trousers, trail boots. The Atlantic Ocean and dramatic cliffs are in the background — a true Wild Atlantic Way landscape (the Slieve League or similar). Overcast or dramatic sky with breaks of light. She is looking at the horizon or the trail ahead, not at the camera. Documentary-style, cinematic wide crop.
prompts.negative_prompt: posing for camera, studio setup, sunny Mediterranean landscape, generic outdoors stock, fashionable gear over practical gear
prompts.style_notes: Quote context: 'Six days along the Wild Atlantic Way with my Trail 28L. Never checked a bag. Never wished I'd packed differently.'
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Aoife's story is the Trail line's testimonial. The Rain Cover Module also needs visual representation — this is it.

prompts.section: Tribe member cards — Marco Lazzarini, Digital Nomad, Barcelona
prompts.purpose: Portrait/lifestyle card for Marco — digital nomad working from Barcelona with Travel 30L
prompts.emotional_role: The life upgrade — this is what 14 countries with one bag looks like
prompts.commercial_role: Drives Travel 30L + Tech Organizer + Power Bank bundle through digital nomad testimonial
prompts.alt_text: Marco Lazzarini, Digital Nomad, Barcelona — TrailVolt Tribe member
prompts.short_prompt: Italian man in his 30s working at a Barcelona terrace café, Travel 30L backpack beside him, Mediterranean light, digital nomad lifestyle, cinematic wide crop.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic wide portrait (2:1) of Marco — an Italian man in his early-to-mid 30s, creative, relaxed, clearly at home in this nomadic lifestyle. He is seated at an outdoor terrace café in Barcelona — Barcelona architecture visible in the background (Eixample facades, geometric street pattern, Mediterranean light). He has a laptop open, the Travel 30L in Core Black or Slate Grey is beside him on the table or hung on his chair, the Tech Organizer Module visible in the open front pocket. Warm Barcelona light — afternoon golden hour. He is focused on his laptop or looking out at the street. Not posing. The image feels like it was taken candidly during a real work session.
prompts.negative_prompt: cold or rainy weather, generic coffee shop, UK or Northern European setting, tourist pose with camera, stressed digital nomad, excessive tech gear spread
prompts.style_notes: Quote context: '14 countries in 11 months. My Travel 30L with the Tech Organizer and Power Bank has never once been checked. It's my office and my wardrobe.'
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Marco's story is the Travel line's most powerful testimonial. 14 countries, never checked — the image must make that feel real.

prompts.section: Tribe member cards — Sarah Kim, Product Designer, Dublin
prompts.purpose: Portrait/lifestyle card for Sarah — commuting through a multi-mode Dublin day with Everyday 22L
prompts.emotional_role: Relatable urban life — gym to office to dinner without repacking
prompts.commercial_role: Drives Everyday 22L + Shoe Module purchase through multi-mode lifestyle testimonial
prompts.alt_text: Sarah Kim, Product Designer, Dublin — TrailVolt Tribe member
prompts.short_prompt: Korean-Irish woman cycling through Dublin city in the morning, Everyday 22L black pack on her back, canal route, cinematic wide crop, golden hour Dublin light.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic wide portrait (2:1) of Sarah — a Korean-Irish woman in her late 20s, stylish and practical, clearly a Dublin urban professional. She is cycling along the Grand Canal or a Dublin cycling lane — Dublin Georgian architecture and canal trees visible. The TrailVolt Everyday 22L in Core Black is on her back — the Shoe Module visible clipped to the bottom or side. She is in motion, not looking at the camera. Morning light — she's heading to work at 6am or early morning. Her cycling gear is smart but not full lycra — she commutes in what she'd wear to the office or gym. Cinematic wide crop. Dublin feels real — not a generic city.
prompts.negative_prompt: suburban location, car-based commute, generic city, posing, modelling posture, pure-sport cycling gear, trails or mountains
prompts.style_notes: Quote context: 'Gym at 6am, office by 9, dinner plans at 7. The Everyday 22L with the Shoe Module handles it all without looking like a gym bag.'
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Sarah's story is the Everyday line's core promise — multi-mode urban life without bag-switching.

prompts.section: Tribe member cards — Tomás Rivera, Trail Runner, Pamplona
prompts.purpose: Portrait/lifestyle card for Tomás — trail runner who also catches Monday flights
prompts.emotional_role: The impossible made real — trails on weekends, airports on Mondays, one pack
prompts.commercial_role: Bridges Trail and Travel line by showing cross-over lifestyle — broadens purchase consideration
prompts.alt_text: Tomás Rivera, Trail Runner, Pamplona — TrailVolt Tribe member
prompts.short_prompt: Spanish man trail running in the Pyrenees foothills, Trail 28L pack, technical stride, golden morning light, dramatic landscape, cinematic wide crop.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic wide portrait (2:1) of Tomás — a Spanish man in his early 30s, lean and athletic, trail-runner build. He is trail running on a mountain path in the Pyrenees foothills near Pamplona — rocky terrain, dry grass, Spanish mountain landscape. The TrailVolt Trail 28L in Core Black (or Forest Green) is on his back — he's running with it loaded, compression straps tight, the Hydration Sleeve in use (water tube visible). He is in full stride — mid-run, completely in motion. Not jogging for the camera — actually running. Morning golden light. The landscape behind is dramatic — ridgeline, open sky, mountains. Cinematic wide crop. The image tells the story of someone whose gear moves as fast as they do.
prompts.negative_prompt: casual walking pace, posed, city running, flat road, standard road running shoes, generic sport background
prompts.style_notes: Quote context: 'I run trails on weekends and catch flights on Mondays. One pack that works for both — that's what I kept telling myself wasn't possible.'
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Tomás embodies the cross-use promise. His story is about a pack that doesn't force compromises between two modes of life.

prompts.section: Tribe member cards — Nadia Voronova, Freelance Developer, Amsterdam
prompts.purpose: Portrait/lifestyle card for Nadia — Amsterdam-based developer who helped co-design the Power Bank Module
prompts.emotional_role: Community ownership — this person isn't just a customer, she helped build the product
prompts.commercial_role: Drives community engagement and module co-design narrative; Power Bank Module purchase
prompts.alt_text: Nadia Voronova, Freelance Developer, Amsterdam — TrailVolt Tribe member
prompts.short_prompt: Russian woman in her late 20s on an Amsterdam canal bridge, Everyday 22L backpack, laptop just packed away, canal and cycling traffic in background, golden afternoon light.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic wide portrait (2:1) of Nadia — a Russian woman in her late 20s, developer, sharp and independent. She is on or near an Amsterdam canal bridge — the distinctive Amsterdam canals, houseboats, cycling lanes, and canal-side architecture clearly visible. The TrailVolt Everyday 22L in Slate Grey or Core Black is on her back; the Power Bank Module is visible clipped to the front face. She is in transition — perhaps walking from one café to the next workspace, laptop in the pack. Looking ahead or down at her phone with purpose. Afternoon golden light, Amsterdam warm tones. Cinematic wide crop. The image captures the independent freelancer's city lifestyle.
prompts.negative_prompt: tourist setting, posed photo op, beaches, tropical setting, generic European city, home office background
prompts.style_notes: Quote context: 'I voted on the Power Bank Module before it launched. When it shipped exactly as I'd hoped, it felt personal. Like I helped build something.' — The image should suggest agency and independence.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Nadia's story is about community co-design — a TrailVolt differentiator. Her story validates the tribe's role in product development.

prompts.section: Tribe member cards — James Thornton, Architect, London
prompts.purpose: Portrait/lifestyle card for James — architect in London whose pack looks better after 1.5 years
prompts.emotional_role: Durability validation — the best endorsement is a pack that ages well
prompts.commercial_role: Addresses purchase hesitation about price vs longevity — this is a worth-it story
prompts.alt_text: James Thornton, Architect, London — TrailVolt Tribe member on London Bridge
prompts.short_prompt: British architect man in his early 40s crossing London Bridge at dusk, Core Black Everyday 22L backpack, suit jacket and quality trousers, cinematic city landscape.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A cinematic wide portrait (2:1) of James — a British man in his early-to-mid 40s, architect, tailored but not stuffy. He is crossing London Bridge or the Millennium Bridge in London at dusk — the London skyline (The Shard, St Paul's, Thames) visible in the background. The TrailVolt Everyday 22L in Core Black is on his back — it looks well-worn in the best sense: the ballistic nylon has patina, the zippers are smooth from use, it sits perfectly on his frame. He is wearing quality clothing — a tailored jacket, chinos, leather shoes — and the pack looks completely appropriate. He is walking in the evening — end of a long architecture-filled day. Dusk London light: warm amber sky, Thames reflections. Cinematic wide crop.
prompts.negative_prompt: new-looking pack with tags on, tourist context, posed for camera, casual dress inconsistent with architect, bright tourist London setting
prompts.style_notes: Quote context: 'I've had the same pack for a year and a half. It looks better now than when I got it. The quality is genuinely absurd for the price.' — The image must show the pack looking great after use.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: James's testimonial directly addresses price objections with a longevity argument. The image reinforces that premium gear only gets better.

prompts.section: Modules section — Chest Phone Module card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Chest Phone Module (€29) — magnetic chest-mount for instant phone access
prompts.emotional_role: Convenience made tangible — never dig into your bag for your phone again
prompts.commercial_role: Drives module add-on purchase. The phone module is a high-relevance, low-barrier entry to the ecosystem.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Chest Phone Module — magnetic attachment accessory
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a chest phone mount accessory on dark navy background, module connection hardware visible, slot for phone, electric mint accent lighting.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Chest Phone Module on a dark navy background. The module is a compact, structured pouch designed to clip to the front face of the pack at chest height — it shows the magnetic connection hardware (stainless steel receiver visible on the back of the module). The front face has a clear slot or elastic pocket for a phone — large enough for a modern smartphone. The module is in Core Black with a subtle electric mint detail on the connection hardware. Compact and deliberate in its design — not bulky. Studio lighting, matte finish hardware visible, product photography quality. The attachment mechanism is clearly the key feature — it should be the most visible design element.
prompts.negative_prompt: person wearing it, attached to pack (standalone product shot only), white background, generic phone pouch, bulky design, velcro-heavy construction
prompts.style_notes: Module shots are flat-lay product photography — the connection hardware is the hero feature of each one.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'Magnetic chest-mount for instant phone access. No fumbling. No opening the bag.' The image must make that instantly clear.

prompts.section: Modules section — Tech Organizer Module card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Tech Organizer Module (€35) — cables, adapters, SD cards, pen loop
prompts.emotional_role: Order from chaos — this is what a tidy tech carry looks like
prompts.commercial_role: High relevance for professionals and digital nomads. Second most likely module purchase after phone module.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Tech Organizer Module — cables, adapters, SD card organiser
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a compact tech organiser module on dark navy background, interior visible with organised slots, cable loops, SD card holders, connection hardware.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Tech Organizer Module on dark navy background. The module is shown slightly open or at an angle that reveals its interior — organised slots for SD cards (4-6 slots), a looped cable organiser area (elastic loops for USB cables, earbuds, adapters), a pen loop, and a mesh pocket. The exterior shows the module attachment connector on the back. The overall module is compact — roughly 20cm × 15cm × 4cm, like a premium travel organiser. All interior compartments are visible but not stuffed. One USB cable is coiled in a cable loop. Matte black exterior, bright interior lining visible (the 210D bright lining of TrailVolt products). Dark navy background, studio lighting.
prompts.negative_prompt: messy cables, overpacked interior, cheap plastic zippers, white background, generic organiser bag, attachment hardware not visible
prompts.style_notes: Interior must look ordered and intentional. This module sells on its organisation promise.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'Cables, adapters, SD cards, pen loop. Never untangle your bag again.' Marco (tribe) uses this in combination with the Power Bank for 14-country travel.

prompts.section: Modules section — Power Bank Module card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Power Bank Module (€39) — 10,000mAh with USB-A and USB-C
prompts.emotional_role: Always-on capability — this is the module that keeps you moving
prompts.commercial_role: High value add-on. Tech professionals and travellers have high purchase intent for this module.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Power Bank Module — 10,000mAh USB-A and USB-C
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a sleek integrated power bank module on dark navy background, USB ports visible, capacity indicator, module connection hardware, electric mint LED accent.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Power Bank Module on dark navy background. The module is a slim, rigid power bank unit — not a generic plastic rectangle but a purpose-designed module with the TrailVolt attachment hardware on its back face. The front face shows: USB-A port, USB-C port (passthrough), capacity indicator dots (LED) glowing subtly in electric mint. The form factor suggests 10,000mAh in a slim, modular-ready package. The connection hardware (magnetic + mechanical lock) is visible on the back. Clean matte black surface. Studio lighting catches the ports and mint LED indicator. Dark navy background.
prompts.negative_prompt: generic Anker-style power bank, white background, cheap plastic appearance, too large/bulky (not integrated-looking), no connection hardware visible
prompts.style_notes: The Power Bank Module was community-voted into existence. It must look like the premium realisation of that promise.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Nadia voted for this before launch. The image must show it looks as good as she hoped.

prompts.section: Modules section — Shoe Module card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Shoe Module (€32) — ventilated isolated compartment
prompts.emotional_role: Hygiene and order — gym shoes and your laptop never touch again
prompts.commercial_role: Gym-to-office commuters are a key segment (Sarah Kim). This module makes the Everyday 22L a complete gym bag solution.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Shoe Module — ventilated isolated compartment for gym shoes
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a ventilated shoe module on dark navy background, mesh ventilation panel visible, module connection hardware, isolated compartment design.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Shoe Module on dark navy background. The module is a structured, ventilated compartment — wider and boxier than other modules. The front face shows a large mesh ventilation panel (breathable, visible interior). The module is large enough for a pair of trainers (size 10/EU 44). The connection hardware on the back face attaches it to the pack's front module rail or lower attachment point. The overall module design is deliberate — it looks like a proper shoe bag that integrates into the system, not an afterthought. One trainer can be slightly visible through the mesh to communicate scale and purpose. Dark navy background, studio lighting.
prompts.negative_prompt: plastic zip-lock bag appearance, cheap mesh, generic shoe bag, white background, no module hardware visible, unstructured floppy appearance
prompts.style_notes: Sarah uses this to transition from 6am gym to 9am office. The image must communicate the separation and containment promise.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Site copy: 'Ventilated isolated compartment for gym shoes or post-trail sandals.' The two use cases (gym + trail) reference both Everyday and Trail line buyers.

prompts.section: Modules section — Clothing Organizer Cube card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Clothing Organizer Cube (€25) — compression cube that collapses flat
prompts.emotional_role: Travel efficiency — more clothes, less volume, complete control
prompts.commercial_role: Travel line add-on. Pairs naturally with the Travel 30L for 4-night trips.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Clothing Organizer Cube — compression cube module
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a compression packing cube on dark navy background, one side expanded with folded clothing, one side collapsed flat, module connection hardware.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Clothing Organizer Cube on dark navy background. The cube is shown in a split-state concept: one half visibly expanded and containing neatly folded dark clothing (a folded t-shirt, socks); the other half suggested as compressed/collapsed flat. The module is rectangular and structured — premium packing cube aesthetic. The compression zipper is visible along the edge. The module attachment hardware is visible on one face. The overall feel: this is how you pack a weekend in a modular system. Dark navy background, studio lighting. No text, no labels.
prompts.negative_prompt: white background, messy unfolded clothing, cheap plastic zipper, generic Eagle Creek-style cube, no module hardware
prompts.style_notes: The 'collapses flat when empty' feature should be visually communicated.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The Travel 30L with detachable day bag + Clothing Cube + Power Bank = the complete system for 4-night trips.

prompts.section: Modules section — Hidden Document Sleeve card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Hidden Document Sleeve (€19) — RFID-blocking, flush with back panel
prompts.emotional_role: Security and peace of mind — your passport is invisible to thieves
prompts.commercial_role: High-relevance for Travel line buyers. Low price point = easy add-on purchase.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Hidden Document Sleeve — RFID-blocking slim module
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a slim RFID-blocking document sleeve on dark navy background, passport-width interior visible, minimal design, hidden attachment hardware.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Hidden Document Sleeve on dark navy background. The sleeve is extremely slim — designed to sit flush against the back panel of the pack (it integrates into the back panel zone rather than the front module face). The front face is a clean, smooth matte black panel — almost invisible when attached. The interior is revealed from a slightly open position: a passport-width slot (showing a passport edge for scale), and a narrower credit card organiser section. The RFID-blocking lining is visible on the interior (a slight metallic sheen to the inner material). The overall design communicates invisibility — this is not meant to be seen. Dark navy background, minimal lighting. No text.
prompts.negative_prompt: bright or colourful design, visible label or branding plate, generic passport holder, white background, no module hardware
prompts.style_notes: Site copy: 'RFID-blocking slim sleeve flush against the back panel. Invisible to thieves.' Invisible but present — that is the design brief for the image too.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The Travel line buyer heading through 14 countries (Marco) needs this. The image must make the security promise feel real.

prompts.section: Modules section — Bottle Holder Module card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Bottle Holder Module (€18) — side-swing, retracts flush
prompts.emotional_role: Practical elegance — hydration without sacrificing the pack's clean profile
prompts.commercial_role: Low-cost add-on with high everyday relevance. Trail and Everyday line buyers both need this.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Bottle Holder Module — side-swing retractable bottle holder
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a side-swing bottle holder module on dark navy background, extended position showing swing mechanism, retracted position suggested, water bottle for scale.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Bottle Holder Module on dark navy background. The module is shown in its extended position — the swing arm is out, holding a slim 1L water bottle (a Nalgene narrow-mouth or similar) in the cradle. The swing mechanism (a pivoting arm with elastic or tensioned cradle) is clearly visible. The module attachment hardware connects to the side attachment point on the pack (different from the front face modules — a side rail attachment). The retracted position is suggested by a ghosted or secondary view showing the arm folded flat against the pack side. Dark navy background, studio lighting. The design must read as 'clean when not in use, functional when needed.'
prompts.negative_prompt: floppy side pocket, velcro-only attachment, generic water bottle holder, white background, pack not present (but no full pack needed — just suggest the side attachment)
prompts.style_notes: Site copy: 'Side-swing holder for 1L water bottles. Retracts flush when not in use.' Both states should be communicated.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The Trail line buyer with a Hydration Sleeve may also need external bottle access. The Everyday commuter needs hydration without an ugly side pocket.

prompts.section: Modules section — Rain Cover Module card
prompts.purpose: Product card image for the Rain Cover Module (€22) — stored in base pocket, full coverage in 10 seconds
prompts.emotional_role: Preparedness — you can handle Irish weather at a moment's notice
prompts.commercial_role: Highly relevant for Irish and UK market buyers. Essential for Trail line configuration.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt Rain Cover Module — full-pack coverage in under 10 seconds
prompts.short_prompt: Studio product flat-lay of a packable rain cover module on dark navy background, compacted into its base pocket, partially deployed showing coverage extent, wet-weather gear aesthetics.
prompts.detailed_prompt: Product photography of the TrailVolt Rain Cover Module on dark navy background. The module is shown in two states in one image: the compact folded state (stored in a self-contained base pocket — a small stuffed pouch showing how it lives on the pack) and a partially deployed state (the rain cover partially pulled out, showing its translucent or dark weather-resistant fabric). The rain cover material has a slight DWR texture visible — the same weatherproofing philosophy as the Trail shell. The base pocket attachment mechanism is visible. Studio lighting catches the rain cover's water-resistant surface. The image communicates: this is stored invisibly, deployed in 10 seconds.
prompts.negative_prompt: generic poncho, bright coloured rain cover, white background, fully deployed cover (hard to read product details), no storage pocket
prompts.style_notes: Site copy: 'Tucked in its own base pocket. Full-pack coverage in under 10 seconds.' Aoife used this on the Wild Atlantic Way.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Dublin and Ireland are rain markets. The Rain Cover Module is an essential purchase for the Irish climate — and the image must make that feel immediately obvious.

prompts.section: Open Graph / Social Preview — Homepage
prompts.purpose: Social preview image for when the TrailVolt homepage is shared on Twitter, WhatsApp, LinkedIn, etc.
prompts.emotional_role: First impression from a link share — must stop the scroll and communicate premium immediately
prompts.commercial_role: Drives cold traffic from social shares and press mentions. OG images are the brand's billboard on social media.
prompts.alt_text: TrailVolt — One Pack. Every Horizon. Premium modular backpack system.
prompts.short_prompt: Premium social preview image: TrailVolt Core Pack on dark navy background, electric mint accent lighting, brand positioning as hero shot, horizontal format 1200x630.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A horizontal 1200x630 Open Graph image for the TrailVolt homepage. The TrailVolt Core Pack in Core Black is positioned left-of-centre on a dark navy (#0B1626) background. Electric mint glow from behind the pack. The tagline 'One Pack. Every Horizon.' appears in white text (Montserrat font feel) in the right third of the image. The TrailVolt wordmark appears in the lower-right corner. The pack is lit cinematically — the same studio rim-light quality as the hero product image. The overall composition: this is a statement brand image — not a banner ad, not a landing page screenshot. It must look extraordinary at thumbnail size (600x315 preview) and full size (1200x630). Horizontal cinematic format.
prompts.negative_prompt: busy composition, multiple products, people, white background, generic banner layout, stock imagery, low resolution appearance
prompts.style_notes: The OG image is often the very first visual contact someone has with the brand from a social link — it must do the entire brand in one frame.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Homepage OG image. This represents TrailVolt on every platform where the site is shared.

prompts.section: Open Graph / Social Preview — Shop page
prompts.purpose: Social preview for the TrailVolt shop page link shares
prompts.emotional_role: The full system in one view — all three lines, all possibilities
prompts.commercial_role: Direct conversion intent — someone sharing or clicking the shop link is already interested; the OG image must confirm quality
prompts.alt_text: Shop TrailVolt — Three pack lines, eight modules. Premium modular backpack system.
prompts.short_prompt: Horizontal OG image: three TrailVolt packs in Core Black, Slate Grey, Forest Green arranged on dark navy background, three lines displayed as a system, electric mint accent details.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A horizontal 1200x630 Open Graph image for the TrailVolt shop page. Three TrailVolt Core Packs are displayed side-by-side in a clean studio arrangement on dark navy background: Everyday 22L in Core Black (left), Travel 30L in Slate Grey (centre), Trail 28L in Forest Green (right). Each is positioned slightly differently to show their distinct form factors. Electric mint rim light on each pack from behind. The shop page headline 'Shop TrailVolt' appears in white in the upper-left or lower bar. The three-line system is evident at a glance. The image communicates system coherence — three packs, one ecosystem. Horizontal cinematic format.
prompts.negative_prompt: single product only, people, white background, modules cluttering the composition, random backgrounds
prompts.style_notes: The shop OG image must communicate the full system at a glance. All three lines in one frame.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Shop page shares happen when people recommend the site — the OG image must show the full scope of the system immediately.

prompts.section: Open Graph / Social Preview — About/Story page
prompts.purpose: Social preview for the About page — founder story shares
prompts.emotional_role: Human and credible — this is a real person's real story
prompts.commercial_role: Brand trust building — converts readers who discovered TrailVolt through press or recommendations
prompts.alt_text: Our Story — TrailVolt. Built out of frustration. Perfected by obsession.
prompts.short_prompt: Horizontal OG image combining founder editorial portrait and TrailVolt Core Pack, Dublin workshop context, brand story positioning, dark navy and mint palette.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A horizontal 1200x630 Open Graph image for the TrailVolt About page. Left half: the founder (Ruslan — male, 30s, Eastern European features, smart-casual) in a Dublin workshop or design studio, looking focused but not posed. Right half or overlay: a TrailVolt Core Pack in Core Black. The headline 'Built out of Frustration. Perfected by Obsession.' appears in white text across the lower portion. The brand name 'TrailVolt' appears in the upper-right corner. The overall image reads as: real founder, real story, real product — the combination that builds trust. Dark navy palette throughout. Warm Dublin light on the founder portrait. The composition feels like a WIRED feature image.
prompts.negative_prompt: corporate headshot, stock person, suit and tie, white background, no product visible, too dark to read at thumbnail size
prompts.style_notes: The About page headline is 'Built Out of Frustration. Perfected by Obsession.' The OG image should carry that energy.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: Press mentions (WIRED, Monocle, Gear Patrol) will link to the About page — this OG image is the brand's editorial identity in one frame.

prompts.section: Open Graph / Social Preview — Tribe/Community page
prompts.purpose: Social preview for the Tribe community page
prompts.emotional_role: Belonging and aspiration — you want to be part of this group
prompts.commercial_role: Community-driven acquisition — existing tribe members sharing this page drive new member sign-ups
prompts.alt_text: The TrailVolt Tribe — 12,000+ adventurers, nomads, and commuters united by one modular system.
prompts.short_prompt: Horizontal OG image showing diverse group of TrailVolt users in different locations — hiker, digital nomad, urban commuter — with packs visible, editorial lifestyle collage.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A horizontal 1200x630 Open Graph image for the Tribe page. A lifestyle collage or editorial split: three zones showing different tribe member contexts — left panel: outdoor/hiking (Wicklow or Atlantic coast), centre: city commuter (Dublin, cycling), right: travel/airport or café (Barcelona, Amsterdam). Each zone shows a person with a TrailVolt pack in the appropriate colourway for their context. The overall image communicates 'diverse community, one system.' The headline 'The TrailVolt Tribe' appears in white text. Community stat '12,000+ members' may appear as a mint-coloured detail. Dark navy overlay unifies the three panels. Editorial photography aesthetic — not collage app style.
prompts.negative_prompt: stock photo diversity display, forced smiling at camera, people posed together, corporate group photo, bright backgrounds without environmental context
prompts.style_notes: The Tribe is a community of real people — the OG image must feel documentary, not stock-photo diverse.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: 12,000+ members in 50+ countries. The image must show why someone would want to belong to this community.

prompts.section: Open Graph / Social Preview — Product Configurator page
prompts.purpose: Social preview for the product configurator — drives direct purchase intent traffic
prompts.emotional_role: Product confidence — the configurator is where you build your pack, and the preview must make that feel exciting
prompts.commercial_role: Highest commercial value OG image — anyone clicking a configurator link is close to purchase
prompts.alt_text: Configure Your Pack — TrailVolt Core Pack. Three lines. Eight modules. Build yours from €159.
prompts.short_prompt: Horizontal OG image: TrailVolt Core Pack with modules being attached, configuration concept, dark navy background, electric mint snap-point glow, 'Build Your Pack' positioning.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A horizontal 1200x630 Open Graph image for the product configurator page. The TrailVolt Core Pack in Core Black is centred. Two or three modules are in the process of being attached — the connection points glow in electric mint at the moment of snap. A selection of modules (Tech Organizer, Power Bank, Phone Module) hover near their attachment points in a configuration composition. The headline 'Build Your Pack' appears in white text in the upper-left. Below it: 'From €159' in mint. The image communicates interactivity and personalisation — not a finished product, but a system being assembled. Dark navy background. Studio lighting.
prompts.negative_prompt: generic product shot identical to shop, white background, all modules attached (finished look), no configuration energy
prompts.style_notes: The configurator page's CTA is 'Build Your Pack' — the OG image must communicate that this is a customisable system, not a fixed product.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: The configurator is the primary conversion page. The OG image drives direct purchase intent when shared.

prompts.section: Open Graph / Social Preview — Sustainability page
prompts.purpose: Social preview for the sustainability page — communicates longevity and responsibility
prompts.emotional_role: Values alignment — this is for the buyer who cares about the ethics of what they purchase
prompts.commercial_role: Converts values-driven buyers who are comparing brands on sustainability — a real differentiator at this price point
prompts.alt_text: Sustainability — TrailVolt. Less. Better. Longer. Built for decades, not seasons.
prompts.short_prompt: Horizontal OG image: TrailVolt pack with spare repair parts arranged beside it on dark surface, electric mint accent, 'Less. Better. Longer.' brand headline, longevity positioning.
prompts.detailed_prompt: A horizontal 1200x630 Open Graph image for the sustainability page. Left: the TrailVolt repair kit arranged on dark surface — YKK zipper pull, Duraflex buckle, module connector, thread spool — lit with electric mint accent. Right: the TrailVolt Core Pack in Core Black, solid and permanent-looking, positioned as the centrepiece. The headline 'Less. Better. Longer.' appears in white text (matching the page hero). Below: 'Built for decades, not seasons.' in smaller text. The brand name in the lower-right corner. The overall image communicates the sustainability philosophy: one quality product that lasts, with parts available for its entire life. Dark navy palette.
prompts.negative_prompt: greenwashing imagery (leaves, generic eco symbols), white background, multiple disposable bags, no product visible, generic nature stock
prompts.style_notes: The sustainability page headline is 'Less. Better. Longer.' The image must carry that minimalist, serious tone.
prompts.why_this_image_fits_trailvolt: 'The most sustainable pack is the one you never need to replace.' That is the brand's sustainability thesis, and the image must express it.
